question.text: If a USDINR call option at strike 83 has a premium of Rs. 0.35 and USDINR spot is 83.20, what is the time value?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Intrinsic = max(83.20 - 83, 0) = 0.20. Time value = Premium - Intrinsic = 0.35 - 0.20 = Rs. 0.15. **The Trap:** The full premium is NOT time value — subtract intrinsic first. **Cross-Exam Context:** Premium decomposition is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Even ITM options have time value before expiry. Only at expiry does time value become zero.
